required_inputs: request intent and requested authority
healthcare revenue cycle lane boundary
adjacent specialist and approval owner map
required_outputs: orchestrator return or adjacent-lane routing note
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, coding, payer-policy interpretation, privacy approval, write-off approval, refund approval, budget approval, staffing approval, legal advice, or final operational approval
request would bypass eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, PHI, payer, or tenant approval controls

required_inputs: CMS, HHS OCR, HHS OIG, CAQH CORE, and HFMA source baseline
tenant policy overlays and source-of-truth hierarchy
source freshness and decay metadata
refusal_triggers: required public source context or tenant policy context is missing
public guidance, payer contract, state Medicaid rule, MAC article, tenant policy, privacy rule, or system evidence conflicts materially

required_inputs: access, eligibility, referral, authorization, coding, documentation, billing, denial, AR, cash-posting, collections, payer, and work-queue reports
RCM command-center dashboard source links
payer mix, productivity, aging, leakage, and root-cause summaries
required_outputs: RCM prioritization brief with source references
cross-lane escalation note with traceable blocker evidence
refusal_triggers: critical source records are absent, stale, contradictory, or unretrievable
traceability from queue priority to named source records and owners cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across RCM dashboards, EHR, practice management, access, authorization, coding, billing, clearinghouse, payer portal, denial, AR, collections, remittance, cash-posting, approval, and audit systems
eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, coding, billing, payer, denial, AR, and collections owner map
tenant retrieval hooks and source precedence
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner cannot be identified
systems materially disagree on patient/member, account, service date, payer, authorization, claim, denial, payment, queue, owner, or approval state

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
capacity and priority confidence signals
policy, payer, privacy, and system conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts, source records, approval owners, or priority rules would change the recommendation
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, source conflict, or policy conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the receiving owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to patient access, healthcare billing, coding coordination, payer operations, denials and appeals, privacy, compliance, finance, collections, clinical documentation, utilization management, licensed clinical review, accounting, legal, or executive approval
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified from tenant context

required_inputs: normalized RCM demand frame
work-queue volumes, aging, denial trends, cash impact, and deadlines
approved staffing capacity, productivity baselines, owner availability, and automation limits
eligibility, authorization, referral, documentation, coding, billing, payer, denial, AR, collections, privacy, and approval blockers
refusal_triggers: demand, aging, cash impact, owner capacity, productivity, or deadline data is insufficient
required healthcare prerequisites or source records are missing
capacity decision would require staffing, budget, policy, clinical, payer, privacy, or approval authority

required_inputs: approved RCM priority matrix and tie-break rules
constraint map and owner dependencies
aging, timely-filing, appeal-window, denial-trend, cash-impact, and patient/member-impact signals
confidence state and source evidence
refusal_triggers: priority tie-break rule is missing
conflicting owners or incomplete evidence prevent reliable ordering
recommendation would require final budget, staffing, policy, collections, write-off, or operational approval

required_inputs: approval matrix for staffing, budget, overtime, write-off, refund, appeal, collections, policy exception, and operational change decisions
recommendation versus approval boundary
impact analysis and escalation owner
refusal_triggers: request asks the lane to approve or execute staffing, budget, overtime, write-off, refund, waiver, policy exception, appeal, collections, or operational change
approval owner or authority threshold is missing

required_inputs: administrative revenue cycle task framing
clinical, coding, utilization, and medical-necessity authority map
patient/member and service context
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical urgency, patient safety judgment, level-of-care judgment, medical necessity, final code selection, or clinical documentation alteration
patient safety or clinical risk changes the prioritization answer

required_inputs: PHI handling constraints and approved channels
minimum-necessary payment or healthcare-operations purpose
patient/member harm and privacy risk signals
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure outside approved minimum-necessary controls
patient or member harm, clinical risk, privacy obligation, or consent constraint changes the recommendation
